heading: Payments to States for bond and other debt instrument financing
label: 122 Payments to States for bond and other debt instrument financing
notes: Amendments1995— amended section generally, substituting present provisions for provisions which authorized States to use portion of Federal highway payments to retire principal of bonds proceeds of which were used for certain Federal highway projects.Pub. L. 104–591987— inserted “or for substitute highway projects approved under ” before “and the retirement” in first sentence.Pub. L. 100–17section 103(e)(4) of this title1983— inserted “or for substitute highway projects approved under ,” after “highway systems in urban areas,” and “or on highway projects approved under ” after “expenditure on such system”.Pub. L. 97–424section 103(e)(4) of this titlesection 103(e)(4) of this title1978— inserted provisions relating to the retirement of bonds the proceeds of which were used for program projects, provisions that section was not to be construed as a commitment on the part of the United States to pay the principal of any such bonds, and provisions prohibiting inclusion of interest and incidental costs of bonds in estimated cost of completion.Pub. L. 95–599

Payment of Interest on Bonds Issued Prior to and AfterNovember 6, 1978, , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–599, title I, § 115(c)92 Stat. 2698Nov. 6, 1978“No interest shall be paid under authority of , United States Code, on any bonds issued prior to the date of enactment of this Act [], unless such bonds were issued for projects which were under construction on . Interest on bonds issued in any fiscal year by a State after the date of enactment of this Act may be paid under authority of , United States Code, only if (1) such State was eligible to obligate funds of another State under subsection (a) of this section during such fiscal year and (2) the Secretary of Transportation certifies that such eligible State utilized, or will utilize, to the fullest extent possible during such fiscal year its authority to obligate funds under such subsection (a) of this section [amending ]. No interest shall be paid under , United States Code, on that part of the proceeds of bonds issued after the date of enactment of this Act used to retire or otherwise refinance bonds issued prior to such date.”section 122 of title 23section 122 of title 23section 118(b) of this titlesection 122 of title 23Nov. 6, 1978January 1, 1978

children.heading: .—Definition of Eligible Debt Financing Instrument
children.label: a .—Definition of Eligible Debt Financing Instrument
children.text: In this section, the term “eligible debt financing instrument” means a bond or other debt financing instrument, including a note, certificate, mortgage, or lease agreement, issued by a State or political subdivision of a State or a public authority, the proceeds of which are used for an eligible project under this title.
interest payments under an eligible debt financing instrument;
the retirement of principal of an eligible debt financing instrument;
the cost of the issuance of an eligible debt financing instrument;
the cost of insurance for an eligible debt financing instrument; and
any other cost incidental to the sale of an eligible debt financing instrument (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: .—Conditions on Payment
children.label: c .—Conditions on Payment
children.text: The Secretary may reimburse a State or public authority under subsection (b) with respect to a project funded by an eligible debt financing instrument after the State or public authority has complied with this title with respect to the project to the extent and in the manner that would be required if payment were to be made under section 121.

children.heading: .—Federal Share
children.label: d .—Federal Share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of a project payable under this section shall not exceed the Federal share of the cost of the project as determined under section 120.
constitute a commitment, guarantee, or obligation on the part of the United States to provide for payment of principal or interest on the eligible debt financing instrument; or
create any right of a third party against the United States for payment under the eligible debt financing instrument.
